summary:
Lysozyme is abundantly present in human tears, serving as one of the highest-concentration proteins in body fluids due to its essential role in defending against microbial threats. Its localized production by the lacrimal gland and significant decline in disorders such as dry eye syndrome further support this finding.  

$\boxed{\text{Yes}}$ 

The final answer is $\boxed{\text{Yes}}$.  

---

(End summary)

Keep strictly factual and concise.
